description: App/monetization KPIs (ARPU family, DAU/MAU, CPI, fill rate, eCPM) and supporting delivery/measurement concepts (attribution window, bid strategy, placements, learning phase, optimization event). Rounds out the reference for non-commerce and infrastructure contexts.

ARPPU.name: Average Revenue Per Paying User
ARPPU.definition: Average revenue generated per paying user. Meta KPI for app/game monetization.
ARPPU.formula: Total Revenue / Number of Paying Users
ARPPU.meaning: Monetization depth among users who actually pay; complements ARPU which dilutes by non-payers.

benchmark.note: Category-specific (gaming vs SaaS differ greatly); track trend.

ARPPU.critical: ARPPU below the level needed to clear CAC.
best_practices: Pair ARPPU with payer conversion to read total monetization

ARPU.name: Average Revenue Per User
ARPU.definition: Average revenue across ALL users, paying and non-paying.
ARPU.meaning: Blended monetization per user; the headline app-economics number.
ARPU.warning: ARPU flat while user base grows (monetization not scaling).
best_practices: Decompose ARPU into payer-rate x ARPPU to localise the lever

ARPDAU.name: Average Revenue Per Daily Active User
ARPDAU.definition: Revenue per daily active user per day, including IAP, ads, and subscriptions. Meta KPI for live apps/games.
ARPDAU.meaning: Daily monetization efficiency of the active base; sensitive enough to detect live changes quickly.
ARPDAU.warning: ARPDAU trending down over consecutive days.
best_practices: Use ARPDAU as a daily live-health monetization signal
common_mistakes: Using monthly ARPU when a daily signal is needed

DAU.name: Daily Active Users
DAU.definition: Unique users who logged in and started a session within a 24-hour period.
DAU.meaning: Daily engaged audience size; denominator for ARPDAU and IMPDAU.
best_practices: Track DAU/MAU as a stickiness ratio
common_mistakes: Treating DAU as revenue without ARPDAU

MAU.name: Monthly Active Users
MAU.definition: Unique users active within a month or rolling 30-day period.
MAU.meaning: Monthly engaged base; the stickiness denominator (DAU/MAU).
MAU.warning: MAU flat with rising acquisition (leaky retention).
best_practices: Pair with DAU for stickiness, not in isolation

IMPDAU.name: Impressions Per Daily Active User
IMPDAU.definition: Average ad impressions served per daily active user; a monetization-intensity measure.
IMPDAU.formula: Daily Ad Impressions / Daily Active Users
IMPDAU.meaning: How aggressively the active base is monetised via ads; too high harms retention.

benchmark.note: Balance against retention; over-serving ads churns users.

IMPDAU.critical: Ad load so high it drives measurable churn.
recommendations: Optimise placements for eCPM not just volume
best_practices: Maximise ARPDAU, not raw impression count
common_mistakes: Chasing impressions at the cost of retention

eCPM.name: Effective Cost Per Mille
eCPM.definition: Estimated ad revenue per 1,000 impressions (publisher side). Meta: estimated revenue / impressions x 1000.
eCPM.meaning: Monetization yield of ad inventory; the publisher mirror of advertiser CPM.
best_practices: Optimise eCPM x fill together for total yield

fill_rate.name: Fill Rate
fill_rate.definition: Share of ad requests that were filled with a served ad. Meta: monetized ads vs ad display requests.
fill_rate.meaning: How often inventory is actually monetised; low fill wastes impressions.
best_practices: Balance fill against eCPM — very high fill at low eCPM may underperform

CPI.name: Cost Per Install
CPI.definition: Average cost paid per app install attributed to the ad.
CPI.meaning: Top-line UA efficiency; must be read against post-install LTV/retention, not alone.

benchmark.note: Platform/geo/genre specific; iOS typically higher than Android.

best_practices: Optimise to value/retention events, not raw installs
common_mistakes: Buying cheap installs that never retain
notes_edge_cases: SKAdNetwork/AEM constraints limit granular install attribution on iOS.

attribution_window.name: Attribution Window
attribution_window.definition: The period after a click or view during which a conversion can still be credited to the ad. Motion example: a 7-day click window.
attribution_window.meaning: Defines how much credit an ad receives; longer windows attribute more, shorter windows are more conservative.
recommendations: Prefer click-based for decisions; validate with incrementality
best_practices: State the window on every ROAS/CPA figure
notes_edge_cases: Post-iOS14 windows shortened; default moved to 7-day click / 1-day view.

view_through_conversion.name: View-Through Conversion
view_through_conversion.definition: A conversion credited to someone who saw but did not click the ad, within the view window.
view_through_conversion.meaning: Captures awareness-driven influence but easily overstates incrementality.

benchmark.note: Treat as supplementary; discount heavily for decisions.

view_through_conversion.warning: VTC forming a large share of reported conversions.
view_through_conversion.critical: Scaling decisions based mainly on VTC.
possible_causes: Broad reach inflating coincidental view credit
recommendations: Separate click vs view conversions in reporting
best_practices: Decide on click-based; use VTC only as directional context

incrementality.name: Incrementality (Lift)
incrementality.definition: The conversions that happened BECAUSE of the ads versus what would have happened anyway, measured by comparing exposed vs a holdout group.
incrementality.formula: (Exposed Conversion Rate - Holdout Conversion Rate) * Exposed Population
incrementality.meaning: The truest measure of ad value; corrects attribution over-credit.

benchmark.note: Run periodic lift studies; compare to attributed ROAS.

incrementality.warning: Attributed ROAS high but lift low (cannibalising organic).
incrementality.critical: Near-zero incremental lift on significant spend.
recommendations: Reallocate from low-lift to high-lift tactics
best_practices: Treat incrementality as the arbiter when attribution and MER disagree
notes_edge_cases: Retargeting often shows high attributed ROAS but lower incrementality.

bid_strategy.name: Bid Strategy
bid_strategy.definition: The rule governing how Meta bids in the auction: Lowest Cost (maximise results in budget), Cost Cap (hold a target cost), Bid Cap, or ROAS Goal.
bid_strategy.meaning: Controls the volume-vs-efficiency trade-off. Lowest Cost maximises spend/volume; caps protect efficiency at the risk of under-delivery.

benchmark.guidance: Start Lowest Cost to find the auction, then layer caps to control cost at scale.

bid_strategy.warning: Cost cap set too tight causing under-delivery.
bid_strategy.critical: Bid strategy starving delivery so ad sets never exit learning.
best_practices: Let learning complete before tightening caps
common_mistakes: Over-constraining bids early and killing delivery

optimization_event.name: Optimization Event
optimization_event.definition: The specific action Meta's delivery is told to maximise (purchase, lead, install, messaging conversation, etc.).
optimization_event.meaning: Determines who Meta finds. Optimising the wrong event mis-trains delivery toward low-value users.

benchmark.guidance: Optimise the deepest event with sufficient volume (~50/week); otherwise step up to a higher-volume proxy.

optimization_event.warning: Optimising a shallow event (e.g. ViewContent) when purchases are the goal.
optimization_event.critical: Volume too low on the chosen event to exit learning.
recommendations: Pick the deepest event with ~50/week volume
Use event prioritisation under privacy limits
best_practices: Graduate to the purchase/value event as volume grows
common_mistakes: Optimising cheap shallow events and acquiring low-intent users
notes_edge_cases: Aggregated Event Measurement limits the number of optimisable events per domain on iOS.

learning_phase.name: Learning Phase
learning_phase.definition: The early period after launch or a significant edit when Meta's algorithm is still determining whom to show the ad to. 'Learning Limited' warns of insufficient conversions to stabilise.
learning_phase.formula: exits at ~50 optimisation events per ad set per 7 days
learning_phase.meaning: Performance is unstable and unrepresentative during learning; edits reset it.
learning_phase.warning: Ad set stuck in Learning Limited.
learning_phase.critical: No ad set ever exits learning (budget too fragmented).
recommendations: Consolidate ad sets; raise budget; reduce edits; choose a higher-volume event
best_practices: Avoid edits during learning; let it complete before judging

spend.name: Spend (Amount Spent)
spend.definition: Total amount charged for delivering the ads in the reporting window. Stored in Adlytic as minor units via the resolved currency factor.
spend.meaning: The input to every efficiency ratio; only meaningful against results.

benchmark.note: Not a performance metric; pace against budget and results.

spend.warning: Spend pacing far ahead of results (burn) or far behind budget (under-delivery).
spend.critical: Rapid spend with near-zero results (see emergency bleed rule R017).
recommendations: Pace spend to results; set guardrails; monitor velocity
best_practices: Always evaluate spend per result, never spend alone
notes_edge_cases: IQD spend is scaled with factor 1 (no minor unit) and Math.round drops fractional IQD, which is correct for that currency.

placement.name: Placement
placement.definition: Where the ad is served: Feed, Reels, Stories, Marketplace, Audience Network, etc. Performance and cost vary by placement.
placement.meaning: A key breakdown dimension; the right creative format must match the placement (vertical for Reels/Stories).

benchmark.guidance: Start with Advantage+ placements; review by-placement breakdown to prune waste.

placement.warning: One placement consuming budget at poor efficiency.
placement.critical: Creative format mismatched to placement (e.g. square in Reels) tanking performance.
recommendations: Match creative aspect ratio to placement
Prefer Advantage+ unless control is needed
common_mistakes: Judging a placement with the wrong creative format
notes_edge_cases: Adlytic stores per-placement metrics in breakdown_stats (publisher_platform / platform_position).
